RECIPES FROM THE TECHCORP KITCHEN
Collected by the Office Culture Committee

TABLE OF CONTENTS
1.  Monday Morning Smoothie
2.  Quick Pasta Primavera
3.  Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
4.  Spicy Black Bean Soup
5.  Avocado Toast (Three Ways)

---

RECIPE 1: MONDAY MORNING SMOOTHIE
Prep Time: 5 minutes | Servings: 2

INGREDIENTS:
- 1 cup frozen mixed berries (blueberries, strawberries, raspberries)
- 1 ripe banana
- 1 cup plain Greek yogurt
- 1 cup milk (any kind)
- 2 tablespoons honey or maple syrup
- 1 tablespoon chia seeds
- 1 handful spinach (you won't taste it, promise)

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Add all ingredients to a blender.
2. Blend on high for 45-60 seconds until smooth.
3. Taste and adjust sweetness if needed.
4. Pour into glasses and serve immediately.

TIP: Prep smoothie packs on Sunday by dividing the fruit and spinach into freezer bags.
In the morning, just dump the bag into the blender with the yogurt and milk.

---

RECIPE 2: QUICK PASTA PRIMAVERA
Prep Time: 10 minutes | Cook Time: 15 minutes | Servings: 4

INGREDIENTS:
- 400g penne or fusilli pasta
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 medium zucchini, diced
- 1 red bell pepper, sliced
- 1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
- 1/2 cup heavy cream
- 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
- Fresh basil leaves, torn
- Salt and pepper to taste

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook pasta according to package directions.
   Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.

2. While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
   Add garlic and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.

3. Add zucchini and bell pepper. Cook for 4-5 minutes, stirring occasionally,
   until vegetables are tender-crisp.

4. Add cherry tomatoes and cook for 2 more minutes until they start to soften.

5. Reduce heat to medium-low. Add heavy cream and stir to combine.

6. Add drained pasta to the skillet. Toss with vegetables and cream.
   Add pasta water as needed to achieve desired consistency.

7. Remove from heat. Stir in Parmesan cheese and torn basil.
   Season with salt and pepper to taste.

8. Serve immediately with extra Parmesan on top.

---

RECIPE 3: CLASSIC CHOCOLATE CHIP COOKIES
Prep Time: 15 minutes | Chill Time: 30 minutes | Bake Time: 12 minutes | Yields: 24 cookies

INGREDIENTS:
- 2 1/4 cups (280g) all-purpose flour
- 1 teaspoon baking soda
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup (226g) unsalted butter, softened
- 3/4 cup (150g) granulated sugar
- 3/4 cup (165g) packed brown sugar
- 2 large eggs, room temperature
- 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
- 2 cups (340g) chocolate chips (semi-sweet or a mix of milk and dark)

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.

2. In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.

3. In a large bowl, cream together softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar
   until light and fluffy, about 3-4 minutes with an electric mixer.

4. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in vanilla extract.

5. Gradually mix in the flour mixture until just combined. Do not overmix.

6. Fold in chocolate chips with a spatula.

7. For best results, refrigerate dough for 30 minutes. This prevents spreading and
   develops a deeper flavor.

8. Scoop rounded tablespoons of dough onto prepared baking sheets, spacing them 2 inches apart.

9. Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until edges are golden but centers still look slightly soft.

10. Let cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.

SECRET: Add a pinch of flaky sea salt on top of each cookie right after baking for
the perfect sweet-salty combination.

---

RECIPE 4: SPICY BLACK BEAN SOUP
Prep Time: 10 minutes | Cook Time: 25 minutes | Servings: 6

INGREDIENTS:
- 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
- 1 large onion, diced
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 jalapeño, seeded and minced (keep seeds for extra heat)
- 2 teaspoons ground cumin
- 1 teaspoon smoked paprika
- 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper (adjust to taste)
- 3 cans (15 oz each) black beans, drained and rinsed
- 1 can (14 oz) diced tomatoes
- 4 cups vegetable broth
- Juice of 2 limes
- Fresh cilantro, chopped

TOPPINGS (optional):
- Sour cream or Greek yogurt
- Avocado, diced
- Tortilla chips
- Shredded cheese
- Hot sauce

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Heat oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion and cook for 5 minutes
   until softened.

2. Add garlic, jalapeño, cumin, smoked paprika, and cayenne. Cook for 1 minute
   until fragrant.

3. Add black beans, diced tomatoes (with their juice), and vegetable broth.
   Stir to combine.

4. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.

5. Using an immersion blender, blend about half the soup for a creamy texture
   with some whole beans remaining. (Alternatively, transfer half to a blender,
   blend, and return to the pot.)

6. Stir in lime juice. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

7. Ladle into bowls and top with your choice of toppings.

MAKE-AHEAD: This soup tastes even better the next day. Store in the refrigerator
for up to 4 days or freeze for up to 3 months.

---

RECIPE 5: AVOCADO TOAST (THREE WAYS)
Prep Time: 5-10 minutes | Servings: 1 per variation

BASE INGREDIENTS:
- 2 slices good bread (sourdough, whole grain, or multigrain)
- 1 ripe avocado
- Juice of 1/2 lemon or lime
- Salt and red pepper flakes

TOAST THE BREAD:
Toast bread until golden and crispy. This is the foundation - don't skip the good bread.

MASH THE AVOCADO:
Cut avocado in half, remove pit, and scoop into a bowl. Mash with a fork to your
preferred consistency (chunky or smooth). Add lemon/lime juice and salt.

VARIATION 1: CLASSIC
- Spread mashed avocado on toast
- Sprinkle with red pepper flakes
- Drizzle with olive oil
- Optional: everything bagel seasoning on top

VARIATION 2: MEDITERRANEAN
- Spread mashed avocado on toast
- Top with: halved cherry tomatoes, crumbled feta cheese, sliced cucumber
- Sprinkle with: dried oregano, sumac
- Drizzle with: balsamic glaze

VARIATION 3: EGG & AVOCADO
- Spread mashed avocado on toast
- Top with: a fried or poached egg (runny yolk is essential)
- Sprinkle with: salt, pepper, red pepper flakes
- Optional: sliced radishes, microgreens, or hot sauce

THE DEBATE: Should you mash the avocado on the toast or spread the avocado halves directly?
We tested both. Mash gives you even coverage and seasoning distribution. Sliced gives you
beautiful presentation. Choose your priority.
